angry.shoggoth.bad: A Shoggoth that keeps slaying others will eventually turn upon its master.
bauble.cooldown: The Bauble requires %1$s more seconds of cooldown.
beginning.netherreturn: A new book lies in my hands, ready to be written. Al Azif I shall call it.
block.beyondtheveil.fume_spreader.crafti: Breathing its fumes while asleep will alter the behaviour of my dreams.\1Must be placed on the ground near a bed or similar object. It must then be filled with {§noniric incense§r}[link:crafting.oniric_incense] and, finally, with a {§nmemory phial§r}[link:crafting.memory_phial] containing the desired memory.\1It can be {§nemptied§r}[caption:Shift + Right Click], but doing so will not return the memory phial.
block.beyondtheveil.memory_sieve.craftin: A fundamental device in the art of manipulating memories.\1An item {§nevoking a certain memory§r}[caption:memories] must be placed on it. Then, an {§nempty phial§r}[link:crafting.memory_phial] may be used on it to capture the memory.
block.beyondtheveil.sleep_chamber.crafti: A complex device known to mimic the dream state induced by sleeping.\1To use it, I must open its door, walk inside and close it while enclosed within.
canoe.fishing.end: I think I've gained enough fish. I should eat enough slugs, then commune with the Idol.
canoe.fishing.start: A fish? From nowhere? Did it jump in or...          ...I should keep at this for a while.
contact.error.night: I must wait for the darkest hours of night to make contact.
contact.error.players: I must be alone to make contact.
dagon.bringgold: These three golden blocks must be brought to the Fisherman Dwellers. We should make haste.
dagon.endbath: We must bathe in the End, and there we must Dream.
dagon.final.2: YOUR DEATH WILL COME IN ITS OWN TIME
dagon.greeting.0: IT IS NOT ALLIANCE I SEEK
dagon.greeting.3: UNSPEAKABLE CONSEQUENCES WILL FOLLOW YOUR FAILURES
dagon.greeting.5: BRING THIS GOLD TO THE DWELLERS
dagon.greeting.6: SO THAT THEY MAY KNOW YOU AS MY AMBASSADOR
dagon.judgement.2: SLAY THREE OF MY ELDER GUARDIANS
dagon.killkeeper.3: AND I WILL GRANT YOU SOME TRUTH
dagon.lastwords: A necessary murder. Should I listen to his last words?
dagon.seaborn.0: ALL LIFE IS BORN FROM WATER
dagon.seaborn.1: THROUGH BLOOD THEY ARE LINKED TO THE DEEP
dagon.seaborn.3: YOU MUST BATHE IN THE LAND OF ENDERMEN
dagon.seaborn.4: ENGULFED IN THE VOID AND CLOSEST TO GOD
dagon.seaborn.5: THERE YOU WILL DREAM WHILE IN WATER
dagon.seaborn.6: AND THE OCEANS WILL BE YOURS
dagon.slayguardians: Three Elder Guardians, in their ancient ocean monuments. A trifle, right?
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.absolut: Absolutely. The peace and quiet of one's own thoughts is the truest bliss.
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.am.opti: Am I wasting time, here, talking to myself?
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.as.opti: As is common, truth lies in the middle. A balance is needed.
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.but.opt: But not too often, lest I really start talking to myself.
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.indeed!: Indeed! Only by reflecting on one's own self can one begin to understand what makes us who we are.
Indeed! Only by reflecting on one's own self can one begin to understand what makes us who we are.
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.it.opti: It won't matter. Deep down, we cannot change who we are.
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.no_.opt: No. Nobody should claim to have all the answers: only by learning from others can we understand the full breadth of humanity.
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.no__.op: No. Civilization is built on unity – we cannot retreat into our own thoughts forever.
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.no___.o: No. Nobody should claim to have all the answers: only by learning from others can we understand the full breadth of humanity.
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.of.opti: Of course it has. The greatest thinkers worked alone.
Yes. Of course. The plight of the shoremen should not remain unheard, and so too my story shall one day be told. Humanity is built on the sharing of thought.
Only... it is exhausting. I should not forget to retreat into my own mental abode from time to time.
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.self.0: Ah, of course! Rise then, oh socially inept! Our time has come!
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.self.1: (But has it really? Will it ever?)
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.start.0: There is just me here. The small fortress that is my mind.
dialogue.black_mirror.after_weep.waste.0: Why so? When quiet introspection can teach so much, what need is there to talk to others?
dialogue.black_mirror.idle.start.0: There is just me here. The small fortress that is my mind.
dialogue.black_mirror.initial.start.0: There is nothing here. A mirror so black I cannot even see myself.
dialogue.black_mirror.initial2.must.0: A tear is born of fear. From the past, present or future. And the shoremen fear what has happened and may happen once again.
dialogue.black_mirror.initial2.must.1: The pain they endure is tremendous.. but why me? Why need I atone the sins of my forefathers?
Sadness, and the inability to explain it.
dialogue.black_mirror.initial2.start.0: There is just me, here. The small fortress that is my mind.
A terrible thought to hold on to, yet hold on I must.
Have I erred? A grief so great — not of a person, but of a *people*. To accept a burden so heavy.. may shatter me.
Too late to turn back. My vision blurs, and a tear caresses my cheek. Such a little thing, yet of such incalculable weight.
dialogue.black_mirror.initial2.that.opti: That I will never make a difference.
dialogue.black_mirror.initial2.that_.opt: That humankind will never make a difference.
dialogue.black_mirror.initial2.weep.0: What is it I fear most?
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.(dr_.o: (Dr. Randolph Carter, of the Miskatonic) "... and I opened the silvered gate, witnessing the unimaginable. We are nothing to infinity."
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.(saint: (Saint Adelina) "A single life is inconsequential – unless its cessation changed the multitude it lied within. Then it becomes sacred, in its finality."
(Saint Jerome) "My sword weighs terribly, yet wield it I must. In choosing the lesser evil, I will do good – but I shall never claim heroism."
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.(the.o: (The Madman of the Sands) "I am a soft grain among dunes. Wind will sweep me away, as if I had never been."
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.adelin: Adelina does not hide her belief in sacrifice for the greater good. Interestingly, she is said to have lived multiple lives, and that in the last few years she has been tormented by with regret.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.but.op: But how would other humans see me, then? I would do this for the good of mankind, but they'd take me for a psychopath!
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.but_.o: But how can one live with hands stained in blood?
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.great_: What do the great minds of history have to say?
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.humans: Really? In the face of all that we have witnessed? Humans are not special. You think yourselves kings of the world and a step above your fellow animals, yet it is only the second step of a stairway that has no end.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.i.opti: I must still think about this. (End)
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.i_.opt: I must still think about this. (End)
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.i__.op: I must still think about this. (End)
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.i___.o: I must still think about this. (End)
I would be remembered not as a scientist, but as a murderer.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.i_____: I must still think about this. (End)
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.in.opt: In truth, I do not have a choice. But I will treat my patients with dignity, as their names enter the annals of history. (End)
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.in_.op: In truth, I do not have a choice. But I will treat my patients with dignity, as their names enter the annals of history. (End)
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.in__.o: In truth, I do not have a choice. But I will treat my patients with dignity, as their names enter the annals of history. (End)
In truth, I do not have a choice. But I will treat my patients with dignity, as their names enter the annals of history. (End)
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.in____: In truth, I do not have a choice. But I will treat my patients with dignity, as their names enter the annals of history. (End)
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.insani: You have killed before. Creatures of all sorts. Life is not holy.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.jerome: Jerome is a warrior turned saint. Though tormented by regret, he still took lives – he knew it had to be done. For the greater good.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.life's: Life's sanctity is exalted and overstated by man's search for meaning, but the two are fundamentally incompatible. One must be ready for sacrifice.
Life's sanctity is exalted and overstated by man's search for meaning, but the two are fundamentally incompatible. One must be ready for sacrifice.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.madman: The mad desertman wrote much on the futility of life, even his own. He passed away by willingly imbibing poison.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.morons: Massively influential morons, then. They shaped the modern cultural zeitgeist into one embracing nihilism, or worse, cosmicism. No one will decry the sacrifice of individual lives.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.need.0: Animals die all the time. Horribly.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.no.opt: No amount of rationalization, explanation and subsequent exculpation could ever justify life's desecration in the way that has been brewing in my cruel, wicked mind.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.no_amo: Oh, great start. Want to try again?
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.no_hes: Of course. Human lives are fleeting in the face of eternity, yet their sacrifice, in the noble pursuit of human knowledge, will be §oimmortal§r.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize.randol: Before his disappearance in recent years, Dr. Carter espoused his pessimistic views on the insignificance of human lives. To him, it would not have mattered.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize2.i.opt: I must still think about this.
dialogue.black_mirror.rationalize2.in.op: In truth, I do not have a choice. But I will treat my patients with dignity, as their names enter the annals of history.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.§odeceiv: §oDeceiver!§r Your experiment could have costed a life!
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.§omadman: §oMadman.§r It will be your blood staining the ground.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.alas,.op: Alas, I'm forgetful. But you are right, I also wish I could.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.and_.opt: And I am quite certain you are not.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.and__.op: And what do you demand in return?
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.answer.0: In our letter we spoke of a partnership – we share a mutual interest – and though we seldom make offers to those outside our aggregation – for safety, of course – you have caught our attention like no one before – bar, perhaps, a single exception.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.deceiver: A worthy sacrifice – that did not come to pass – and in so daring, we proved your true nature – the blood coursing through your veins.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.demand.0:
We are fascinated by the relation between life and death, between birth and passing – and what we ask in return is a §onewborn§r.
It does not have to be a human – any animal, from cat to horse, will do fine. Build two pillars: any baby born near to the first will signal us to bring a human near to the second.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.demand.1: That, then, is our trade. A newly born life in exchange for one soon to expire – at your hands, if you choose to.
I shall embrace death – just as I did life.
Nevertheless, know that – on behalf of my brothers and sisters – I come in peace, and with an offer.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.exceptio: Saint Adelina, of course – Jerome's virtuous consort, who was said to have lived a thousand lives – she is immortal, just like you.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.forgetfu: We – nonetheless – see you as an important stepping stone in the long path of humankind – a fulcrum, perhaps – and a long, eventful life.
Hm-hm. Alright, tell me what you want.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.hm-hm___: Hm-hm. Alright, tell me what you want.
Hm-hm. Alright, tell me what you want.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.horrible:
You are now playing in the economy of lives, dear – just like we are – and for the betterment of all humanity, it must continue.
If you wish to proceed, simply build the two pillars. We will be there to celebrate the beginning of our partnership.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.hypocris: Oh, drop the hypocrisy! You rip hearts and spines, experiment on hapless fools – you are in no place to make such noble statements, white knight – preach not what you do not follow.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.i'm.opti: I'm not "undying". I just come back after death.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.i.option: I hope it's worth the blood you just spilled.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.offer.0: We are an aggregation of scholars – and merchants – bound by a common fascination of the links chaining us all – brother and sister – man and beast – life and death.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.offer.1: We wish to understand these relations – they are born and manifested in blood – and have devoted ourselves to studying – just as you have – so that we may understand that insurmountable threshold that is death.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.offer.2: §o* The cultist's voice changes slightly at every pause *
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.study.0: You have struck us, yes – undying as you are – like no one has ever done before – bar, perhaps, a single exception.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.tell.0:
Our proposed partnership is a business one – we trade in unorthodox goods, that may be of great help to your research.
What we offer is §ohuman lives§r, folks plucked from their villages and left in your hands – to do with them what you desire.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.tell.opt: Tell me why you have come here.
Thanks, I guess, but please tell me what you want.
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.then.opt: Then tell me, what do you demand in return?
All the more compelling! You have been at both sides of the threshold, and oh if you could only remember what's on the other end of it!
dialogue.blood_cultist.immortal.you.opti: You have not answered my question.
dialogue.blood_cultist.initial.i_.option: I resurrect from every death, if that is what you are asking.
dialogue.blood_cultist.initial.killed.0:
What worth is mercy when in the way of progress? Nothing – and the curiosity of ones like you – and us – is what drives the world forward.
We will meet once you have something to offer to – and something from demand from – our humble association.
dialogue.blood_cultist.initial.no,.optio: No, and I want nothing to do with you.
dialogue.blood_cultist.initial.respawn.0: Shhh, do not tell – we know already!  We must wait, and play the act anew – once you have something to demand from – and something to offer to – our humble association.
dialogue.blood_cultist.initial.start.0: The chains binding us lie yet unseen – but worry not: all is linked, all is connected, and new life is born from each death – and you know death all too well, do you not?
dialogue.drowned.gnawing.i.option: I shall not speak to it today.
dialogue.drowned.gnawing.start.0: For the first time, I notice it: something crawling at the back of my head.
dialogue.drowned.gnawing.start.1: It is like a pain grown over several weeks or months, until you realize it's not normal and should not be there.
dialogue.drowned.gnawing.start.2: Except... this one has been with me my whole life, in my mind and everyone else's. It is the chaos that crawls within all living beings.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.but.option: But what about other worlds? Ones without water?
dialogue.drowned.ocean.child.0: All life is born from me.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.depths.0:
Travel far from land, sailing on a canoe of the shoremen, holding a gift.
My oldest children will recognize you, and whisper secrets in your mind.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.dreamer.0:
No. I am His child.
And through the Great Dreamer – the Veil itself – infinity is filtered into water, and from water all is born.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.father.0:
The shoremen's dreams travelled through me, before leaving the world.
And they dreamt grateful visions of you, of your compassion.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.how.option: How do I reach the Veil?
dialogue.drowned.ocean.how_.option: How do I reach your depths?.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.human.0:
And so it is to be hateful.
The shoremen were punished for their devotion to the Great Dreamer, by your kin.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.it.option: It is only human to be compassionate.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.last.0:
There was a convergence of thoughts.
You too are multifaceted, though you do not yet know, and your mind is fractured, irreparably.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.last.1: And, of course, that Chaos that Crawls within all living beings, that Messenger who is the Message itself, spoke aloud, for the first time in your life.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.one.option: One last thing: who else entered my mind during my baptism?
dialogue.drowned.ocean.veil.0:
Delve into my depths, far below the earthly crust I bear atop of me.
And let us speak again there.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.veil.1:
Be forewarned, however: I am now calm and placid, but I am multifaceted.
And the next time we meet, I shall be tempestuous.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.waterless.0: I lie beneath all worlds, strewn across the stars.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.who.0: I am the Ocean, eldest in time.
dialogue.drowned.ocean.who.1: I have also been called Arche, the First Principle, and Dagon, the Archpriest.
dialogue.shoreman_bartender.communed1.dr: I... cannot pretend that I know for sure. It is a belief we are taught from youth, but what is a belief standing only on faith?

Many of my brethren now disagree that the Dreamer is aware of us at all – it is a matter of opinion – but we §oknow§r he exists, as sure as the nightly fog, the ignorance of man, and the starry sky dividing us from infinity!
dialogue.shoreman_bartender.communed1.ev: Even within such a small community, you still have and accept a divide of thought. Surprising, yet beautiful.
I see. At least the Ocean listens.
dialogue.shoreman_bartender.communed1.it: It is an honour indeed. Cheers to that!
dialogue.shoreman_bartender.communed1.of: Of course, and let's drink to that!
dialogue.shoreman_bartender.communed1.ou:
A desert man, but he... he was not kind.
I hope for our friendship to be stronger.
dialogue.shoreman_bartender.communed1.so: So the Dreamer was really aware of me?
dialogue.shoreman_bartender.communed1.st: Aha! You heard it too, they say! Father Ocean's rumble and grumble and, through Him, the Great Dreamer's gaze! Only one outsider before has ever had such honour, so you're making history!
dialogue.shoreman_bartender.communed1.ye: Yes, he does listen. If he didn't, would that not make us irrelevant?
dialogue.shoreman_bartender.initial.star:
Hmph. We do not take kindly to outsiders, but I won't deny you a night's rest.
There are beds up above.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.communed1.st:
Hmm. The Ocean spoke to you.
And if you need to reply, you must sail it. Our canoes engender kindness from the sea: I shall share with you our woodwork.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.communed1.th: Thank you. I would be happy to see it.
Thank you. I may need it soon.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.communed2.st: Carpentry's a hard job. Not like fishing – the Ocean brings me no gifts.
But are we not born of the same common ancestor? We both share its blood.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial.comm: No. Our closest common ancestor is water, that most ancient of substances that gave birth to all things, predating and fathering blood.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial.hmph:
Go. Return whence you came.
We have §onothing§r in common.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial.i_.o: I do not "believe". I §oknow§r that I §ocannot know§r.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial.noth: Nothing? We are both humans, born of the same blood, the same ancestors!
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial.olde:
Father Ocean spawned Daughter Earth, and not the other way around.
Your beliefs are apocryphal.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial.sibl: If you seek your siblings, go back to your people. We are not brethren. Hah! Not even cousins!
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial.unkn: Ah, then you believe in the Unknowable! And so do we, deeper even than the ocean.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial.wate: Water is not the oldest substance. Not even close.
Why are you folks so cold! Are we not all brothers and sisters?
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial.yes_: Yes. §oThere§r is something we have in common.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial1.i.o: I have not interest in your damp, creaking lumber.
It is a shame. Your art would be highly valued among my people.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial1.sta: I am a woodworker, build canoes for the fishermen. But my craft is not for you outsiders.
dialogue.shoreman_carpenter.initial1.you: Your woodwork is out and about, what's stopping me from taking it?
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.communed1.i.opti: I am not irrelevant. I am immortal.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.communed1.i_.opt: I would not dare breach your trust, nor lay a hand on any of you.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.communed1.i__.op: I can make no promises – it is outside of my control.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.communed1.outsid: Yes, of course. Just do what the book tells you to do, and if we are in the way, so be it. We are irrelevant to you – just like you, perhaps, are irrelevant to someone else.
Hmph. Perhaps I was wrong about you.
Then again, perhaps not. Perhaps you will betray us, and the good keeper who now so trusts you.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.communed1.the.op: The book doesn't tell me what to do. I tell *it* what I do.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.initial.and.opti: And I may do it again.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.initial.anyone.0:
Hmm.|
Try the lighthouse keeper. He sees the clearest, up there in his tower.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.initial.buy.0: Hmph. Special prices, just for you.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.initial.i.option: I was joking! Was it not obvious?!
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.initial.i_.optio: I do not deny that, in the past, I have not refrained from killing.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.initial.is.optio: Is there anyone willing to speak to me?
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.initial.start.0: Huh? Are you here to buy or to steal?
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.initial.steal.0:
Of course, just arrived and yet you already plunder our resources, stealing from our chests and barrels our hard earned goods.
Because you just §odo not care§r about ones such as us.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.initial.steal.1: That's the truth of it. To you, we are irrelevant. Insignificant. And, given the opportunity, you would wipe us all out| — not out of need, but just because you §ocould§r.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.initial.talk.0: What's there to talk about? You and I are too different, only seemingly speaking the same language, yet in truth our words are incomprehensible to one another.
dialogue.shoreman_clerk.initial1.start.0: Hmph. Special prices, just for you.
dialogue.shoreman_cultist.1: Your friend betrays you. We wish to see the colour of your guts.
dialogue.shoreman_cultist.2: No! I know I must die, but please, do not let me bleed like a pig!
dialogue.shoreman_cultist.3: Ohhh, you wish you bled like a pig, yet your blood is older, and paler!
dialogue.shoreman_cultist.5: You are the misbegotten child of earth and water. Tell us, §owas §oit §oyour §omother §oor §ofather §owho §owore §oscale §ofor §ocloth?
dialogue.shoreman_cultist.7: Then Father Ocean shall embrace your remains.
dialogue.shoreman_cultist.do_not.0: |||Just as I embraced life,||| so too shall I embrace death.
dialogue.shoreman_cultist.do_not.option: The Keeper must die, but on his own terms. §oDo not§r take his life, or you too shall meet your end.
dialogue.shoreman_cultist.go_ahead.optio: If he must die, then it matters not in what manner – the dead do not regret. Go ahead.
dialogue.shoreman_cultist.laugh:
HAHAHA!||
Well, friend – now you have breached the lie| – is that not what you wanted?
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.communed1.clearl: Clearly reaching a breaking point with the booze...
Ach, sea water, thar's ye problem! Away!
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.communed1.some.o: Some water would do you well.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.communed1.was.op: Was touched by the Ocean, it seems.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.communed1.you.op: You are less loquacious than last time.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.communed2.can.op: Can I offer you some water?
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.enough.0: Ooof, why can't ya stooop. Ye go on an' on an' on. Yah thar's why we hate ye. Not cause ye killed me ma. Ye just ask too much.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.i_.option: I don't hate you, and I'm sure my kin wouldn't either.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.i__.option: I only hate you because you hate §ome§r.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.i___.optio: I don't hate you, and I'm sure my kin wouldn't either.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.i____.opti: I only hate you because you hate §ome§r.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.kin.0: Yah but then why ye gotta kill me ma, an' then ye try an' kill me pa, but then me pa kill yer pa, an' wait where was I goin' with this...
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.not.option: Not to sound rude, but why do you all look so unlike the.. other humans of the world?
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.pa.0: Oh betcha he was a good looking fella. Not like mine. Slimy and all.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.saying.0:
Oh yah. What was I sayin'?||
Ah, may'aps somethin' bout us fisherfolk. Yah we a nasty bunch. Pale all over.| Except me nose.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.secluded.0:
Nono, we ain't secluded. We samply hate y'all, thar's all.
Cause y'all hate us, I guess. Dunno.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.sorry__.op: Sorry.. I see you're getting tired.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.start.0: Ain't thar the good stuf-|| §o*hic*§r beg pardon.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.unlike.0: Oi! It's ye who look weird an' all. Thar's why we hate y'all. Ye just cannae stop hatin' §ous§r.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.where.0: Ain't seen 'im in a while. Swimmin' somewhere. Deep down below.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk.why.option: Why are you folks so secluded?
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.drunk1.start.0: ]]Ye just go on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' §o*hic*§r on an' on an' on an' on an' on an' on!!
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.initial.brethren: You'd talk ill too, had ya seen what I seen.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.initial.elaborat:
]]Hmmm.[[
Nah. Cannae talk on a parched throat.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.initial.how.opti: How can you talk so ill of your brethren?
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.initial.mean.0:
It's just.. I dunnae like life here no more. Ne'er did, actually.
Broodin', borin' fishermen. Misbegotten children of earth and water, I tell ya! That's what we be!
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.initial.nothing_: Nothing. I wonder why you'd count me lucky as an outsider?
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.initial.start.0:
A newcomer, eh? We dun't see many outsiders no'adays.
Good for ya. Being an outsider an' all.
Thanks. You look very far from sober, by the way.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.initial1.can't.o: Can't get anything out of a thirsty man.
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.initial1.might.o: Might be good if you don't drink for a while..
dialogue.shoreman_drunk.initial1.start.0: Ach, cannae drink sea water. Ne'er again...
I will most certainly soon be gone.
I hope you can see me as a friend from now on.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.communed1.st:
You... are born from water, just like us!
Yes, we all are! And that is the common ancestor that truly matters!
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.communed1.th: Through the Great Dreamer I see the world in a new light. I thank you for the revelation.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.communed2.hi: His spouse, Adelina, was the truly vicious one.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.communed2.je:
Heh, maybe. For all his misdeeds, he had a great mind.
But you, friend, have both that and kindness.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.communed2.st: Bless! Bless! A blessing upon thee!
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.communed2.th: Thank you. A blessing upon thee as well.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.initial.bego: ]I said begone![ Your kind are §onot§r welcome here!
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.initial.my_k: §oMy§r kind?! Different we may look, we are both fellow humans!
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.initial.no_w: This is no way to treat a weary traveller!
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.initial.prej: Your prejudice will be your downfall.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.initial.same: We are both creatures of flesh and blood. Does that not unite us?
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.initial.sund: We have nothing in common. Whatever bond you may think we share: there is none, and if there had ever been one then it was violently sundered.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.no_closer.id: I get it. You are the village idiot.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.no_closer.ne: I never did. I never §owould§r have.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.no_closer.no:
]]I don't believe you! I don't believe you!
Away! Away!
I swear not to harm you.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.no_closer.re: I do not forget. All my deeds are recorded in §oAl Azif§r.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.no_closer.st: No, no, no! Don't get close, we suffered too much already!
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.no_closer.up:
Deaths upon deaths upon deaths. 
And now you have forgotten.
dialogue.shoreman_fisherman.no_closer.yo: You did. Do you not remember?
dialogue.shoreman_lighthouse_keeper.afte: Just wanted to make sure you are doing well.

I am old, and do not have long left to live.
But I am not afraid, and in passing I know Father Ocean shall embrace my remains.
dialogue.shoreman_lighthouse_keeper.bapt: And then?
Yes. And so will you, I hope.

§oListen,§r Seeker.
We are human, just like all of you – but like any two humans, we also differ, and there are things we cannot do that others can, and viceversa.

That...
I do not know. Perhaps our attunement with water, or the strength of our faith. Things you now share.
Do you remember how it was?
Have you gone through the ritual too?
How was His voice?
I hope so too. Thank you.
I understand. Then please tell me how to perform the ritual, so that I may see whether we are more alike than not.
I thought I was no longer an outsider to you.
I believe so too.
I wish to speak to the Ocean.
I understand. Thank you.
I will keep that in mind. Thank you.
Intriguing...
Just wanted to make sure you are doing well.

Ah, I was afraid you would, eventually.
Baptism is a sacred rite we undergo when young, to enter the next stage of our lives.

Yet, it is dangerous.
Listen: no outsider has ever attempted this, and if they did, they would not survive it.

I am old, and do not have long left to live.
But I am not afraid, and in passing I know Father Ocean shall embrace my remains.
Indeed, and for precisely this reason I think you can make it.

I was terribly, terribly afraid, and when I was put in the water, knowing only that I should not let go, I realized I could not breathe and that my life was running out and I knew for certain that they had lied to me because I could not breathe no matter how hard I tried and I was going to die and be forgotten and all my life had led to nothing and was worthless and then I hear Him and I BREATHE.
In, and out. In. And out. Again. And again.
Welcome back, dear friend.
Slugs are our means of communion. You must consume one, while submerged in water in a small hole surrounded by our dark sand.
And then you drown.
Then tell me. Please.
Then tell me. Please.

Hopefully, it will not all end there.
Yet, sometimes it does, and some of my brethren just let go, so that we never again heard their voice.

Calm and placid.
But, be forewarned: he is not always so.
What makes you different from outsiders?
What?!
Yes, as we all did.
You do not know? Then why proclaim with such certainty that an outsider would die, when none even attempted?
You breathed in water?

Welcome back, dear friend.
I wish you the best for your baptism.
Thank you.
"Saint" Adelina. But one day she vanished all the same.
Adelina?

I do not know either. Perhaps nobody does.
Search texts, ask luminaries, see if anyone went through the same.
I pray for you, friend.
Can you tell me what to expect?

Oh... well, you are ready now.
And I know your bravery, and that you will do so unafraid... but be prudent. Do not offend them.

They are our... friends. And allies.
But... no, words will not do them justice. You must see them yourself. They are wondrous, made in the shape of Father Dagon.
He was not alone. I heard three voices.
He told me to meet his oldest children, sailing out on a canoe.
Yes. But one day she vanished all the same.
How did you know?
I see. Thank you.
I do not think anyone could have gone through the same. I am... unique.
I shall be courteous, then.
I shall be courteous, then.
I see. Thank you.
I spoke with the Ocean.
I heard three voices.
I am also shaken. I do not know what I awakened.
I still spoke to the Ocean, though. He told me to meet his oldest children.
I know that you cannot truly die, Seeker. But you are not the first, and you will not be the last.

We saw it in a dream, afraid that the past would repeat itself.
If you had not come in peace, how could we have stopped you? Just like we did not stop §oher§r.
And blessed be Dagon, then! Embracing you like you embraced us.
You live! Oh, blessings, bless, bless you!
Thank you.
Then I shall be prudent.
Then I shall be prudent.
What?! That has never happened before! No, no, it cannot be!
Who else was like me?
dialogue.shoreman_lighthouse_keeper.comm: Fear not. I have all the time I need.

Once the mind relinquishes its preconceptions, so too does the journey towards truth become lighter.
Do not tarry, Seeker! You have so many wonders yet ahead of you.
Thank you... for opening my mind.
dialogue.shoreman_lighthouse_keeper.did_: A bond to reconcile ancient enmities. I shall.
We are so, so terribly afraid, traveller. Of the past repeating itself. Can you blame us? When staring in the eyes of genocide, how could one respond?

Your ancestors were frightened by our customs, of the black soil that spread beneath our hamlets. They were afraid of our worship and beliefs, terrorized by a deity truer than theirs.
But, perhaps, you may yet break the cycle of agony. Were you to learn,| rather than kill. To be curious,| rather than prejudiced.
I will, because I seek Truth.

I saw you tonight, traveller. Or rather, in my dream I saw yours. Speeding down below, towards the bottom of the sea.
It was a brave, angry dream, seeking an unveiling of truth as if dissatisfied with what you already knew.
When we first met, I spoke of fear,| and of prejudice,| yet chastised you for your heritage. I ask you now to forgive my hypocrisy.
Embracing all of your grief... I don't know if I can.

We are frightened, traveller, and we weep, for tears are born of fear.
Will you weep for us?
Why did you fear me so?
Will you shed a tear for our sake?
dialogue.shoreman_lighthouse_keeper.init:
Ahh, I see. Truly, we are so very proud of what we have built. 
And rebuilt. 
And rebuilt over and over again, just for it to be set ablaze, and rent asunder.

Know you not of what and whom I speak? Of the misdeeds of ages past? Crimes and atrocities, committed against us by self proclaimed 'saints' and 'hunters', blinded by their fear and prejudice.
They were your ancestors, traveller. And just like them, you are not welcome here.

Then §oplease§r, I beg of you...
§oLet no further harm come upon my people.§r
Darkness.
A... dream? A dream of what?
Your accusations are groundless. There are no records of any such "crusades". I cannot trust your words.
I didn't know it would lead me here, so I guess it was chance.
As hard as it is to believe, I was guided here by a dream.

Him? Yes, we chose to portray him in a most striking visage. Amorphous, almost shapeless. Inscrutable.
And, indeed, striking he was, inviting the most violent and ruinous attention.

Know you not of what and whom I speak? Of the misdeeds of ages past? Crimes and atrocities, committed against us by self proclaimed 'saints' and 'hunters', blinded by their fear and prejudice.
They were your ancestors, traveller. And just like them, you are not welcome here.
That idol in the town square.

We are just §ohumans§r!  But §o§lyou§r were fearful of us venerating a different god, and all that he represents.
Because §owe§r worship the Veil, that great final threshold all dreams go through. The clouding of thoughts, the fog in the night, the blindness of man and the prejudice driving you towards unspeakable acts.

And it lives deep down below, at the bottom of the sea. Hiding all sorts of truths, that filter of infinity in the emptiness of the §ovoid§r.
Your ancestors could not tolerate its existence, because it would have shattered all your beliefs.
My mission is for all of mankind, and that includes §oyou§r.
Mere chance.
You must not fear me.
I... was not aware. Our church speaks of "sinners", and of "inhuman heretics". I did not imagine..
I am not my forefathers.

The precursor of light. Rising up from the ocean to blind the eyes of man.
But no, do not worry. Here, atop the lighthouse, you can see §oeverything§r.
Tell me then, traveller. What revelations do you hope to uncover?
I see. There has to be so much you must have seen in your endless journeys. I can only envy you, stuck as I am, tending this lighthouse.
Yet, I am left to wonder, what does an explorer like you think of our humble hamlet? Out of all the beauties you must have admired, what is it that strikes you most?

Oh. A traveller.||| 
Welcome.
How did you stumble upon our hamlet, may I ask? Was it chance? Or did you seek us?
I do not know. I am still exploring, the world and myself.
The unique architecture, how everything fits upon the water on which it's built.
Do not fear.
I cannot trust you.

§oPlease§r... I beg of you...
§oLet no further harm come upon my people.§r
dialogue.shoreman_lighthouse_keeper.than: I admire your resolve. You do not flee the Veil, when you dream nor when you weep. You wish to breach it, go beyond the lies you are told and grown into.

Look at the ocean, traveller. The boundless bulwark concealing truth. The Great Dreamer lies beneath it, the last door before the unknown.
Seek communion, down at the idol in the town center, and adopt our beliefs. Acknowledge the ignorance the human mind is born into| – only then can you overcome it. Farewell, seeker.
Thank you, traveller. Yours is a rare gift. To us, and to yourself.
The old truths were deceits. How can I dispel the dogmas I grew up believing in?
dialogue.shoreman_lighthouse_keeper.want: And I wish to embrace your culture. I would be glad to try one of your white slugs.
I would be glad to try one of your white slugs.
Just wanted to make sure you are doing well.

I am old, and do not have long left to live.
But I am not afraid, and in passing I know Father Ocean shall embrace my remains.

Oh, that is a joy to hear! They are, indeed, sacred to us.
Only, a warning: they are an acquired taste.
Variety is the spice of life.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.communed1.star:
Traveller... you have my gratitude.
And not just that: I feel an innate admiration towards you, and your willingness to overcome those inner biases that gnaw at the mind of every human.
You challenged your own beliefs in pursuit of what is true. That takes tenacity, as few humans would remain unperturbed by the sudden upheaving of their view of the world.

Please, let me help you in any way I can. I am knowledgeable – perhaps my only asset in this world, and my only role in this quest of yours.
Come back to me if you wish to discuss esoteric topics – such as those studied by the academia of the occult, but of which the outside world is blissfully unaware; or the beliefs to which my kin so tightly held even in the face of tragedy.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.communed1.than: Thank you. I will come back.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.accept: I am not surprised. He still holds a lot of love in his heart, despite all he's gone through.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.acquai: But... but I §oam§r acquainted with Al Azif! Though virtually unknown outside of occult academia, it is widely celebrated within our inner circles as the Madman of the Desert's magnum opus, a treasure trove of his arcane findings.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.adelin: Adelina, who has lived a thousand lives. Deemed a "Saint" like her betrothed, yet ruthless, cold-blooded – after all, a single life means so little to her.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.afraid: I do not blame you. Fear is the strongest emotion of mankind, and we know it all too well.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.agains: Yet, what if you had been different back then?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.alazif:
Hah, for a moment I thought you had scribbled your findings on the original!
Though, yes, if you ever seek to publish it, I suggest you change its name.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.alread:
Oh! Oh that is wonderful!
And... and He replied?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.alrigh:
Yes, yes... it's just that...
...
Well, know this: your ancestors travelled to those depths, to §ofight§r.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.and.op: And I wish to ask about you in particular. How do you know so much?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.and_.o: And I wish to ask about you in particular. How do you know so much?
And what is this Veil, then?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.anyone: We are no longer that optimistic.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.apples: Ah, the good ones! And where were these good apples when the others came for us?!
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.are.op: Are the Great Dreamer and the Veil one and the same?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.badthi: Perhaps not indeed, and as you come to terms with the impossibility of it all, you may find yourself comforted in the knowledge that it does not truly matter, and that what is most important is your own perspective.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.baptis:
Each dialogue with the Ocean is a sacred act for us of the Shore, and for the first such occurrence we undergo a secret ritual: a baptism, biting into a slug while in water... until we drown.
It is not something we readily share with outsiders, yet, perhaps, as you embrace more and more of our customs, we will reveal it.
Thank you. You, alone, have shown compassion. We cannot be grateful enough.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.blissf: Blissfully unaware, is what I have been! Only recently did I see through the old dogmas.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.but.op: But even then, there is a veil. Do we truly know ourselves?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.but_.o: But we may yet breach it.
But there has to be a "ground truth" at the bottom of it all! Something we can deconstruct, to map all facts and events as they happened.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.but___: But how? Where can I find these chains?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.can_.o: Can I reach the Veil, in person? Has anyone ever done it?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.cannot: I know that I cannot know – and that very fact proves my statement.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.chains: Blood rituals are a staple practice among many peoples of the world, often involving sacrifice of mortal men and women to obtain boons. It has also been studied by academia, though not extensively, and always at a safe distance.

These rituals are now viewed by researchers as rigidly scientific processes, yet dressed in mysticism and the beliefs of those who practice them. I do not know – nor wish to know – the details – but, if you do, I suggest you contact the responsible department at the Miskatonic.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.change: Nothing. Just that... people change, over the course of long lives.
Changed the title, as you advised.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.concei: It is a matter of opinion, but, yes, I think it is. Indeed, I find it our greatest conceit that we insist on giving ones such as Him shape and form. On what grounds can we reduce Him to our stature?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.conver:
This is not good.
The Gnawing Sensation? The Chaos that Crawls? Had you ever heard those names before the baptism?! Answer me!
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.dagon': Dagon's blessing upon you as well.
Dagon's blessing upon you as well.
I know – for one such as you it would be easy to dismiss it.
But you should fear it nonetheless.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.disapp: Yes, and from there comes my warning: be afraid of death. You never know when it might be your last.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.discom: I can see that too, and how it could prove... frightening. This is the Veil after all, and to know that there is something out there, but that we cannot know what... it is what drives people mad.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.do.opt: Do you find it wrong to personify Him?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.does.o: Does this mean He responds to your prayers? That He listens to us?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.eye.0: The Eye of Knowledge. It succinctly encapsulates the birth of life: from the outermost unknown, to the void, to water, to blood, to you, the pupil in the middle, usually depicted askew to symbolise the degree of freedom that you have, however small, to study what surrounds you.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.eye.1:
That is, of course, only my interpretation, from one pupil to another.
I wonder, if I may ask, where did you first encounter this symbol?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.forgiv: Forgive me, but I'd rather not say.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.fright: That is the nature of dealing with all things involving the Veil, and as we uncover more truths, so too do we understand how little we know.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.gods.0:
Ah, but already we diverge: we believe in a god, not in gods.
And, even then, I would hold qualms with referring to the Great Dreamer as a "god" – correlating Him to the mythical deities pervading your culture fails to capture the intricacies of our faith: we worship the ideal the Veil encapsulates, and the personification in our central square serves only to give the fishermen a feeling of tangibility, for something that is inherently intangible.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.greate:
But then, even discounting the infeasibility of it all, and the intractable effort of amassing all such knowledge, and then absorbing it, even so, would it not still be marred by that final, inescapable bias that is your own, as you read and learn through your own lens, link it to your own events, and react to it as your temperament dictates? Even now as I speak.
Objectivity is unattainable, and all knowledge is inherently subjective.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.ground: Does there have to be? How can you seek a single truth when every living thing has its own perspective of the universe? There are as many truths as there are beating hearts, else the world would not be so divided.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.hadyou: I see. And do you know what you would have done, had you been there?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.hah!.o: Hah! I am quite the stubborn individual – I do not change easily.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.he.opt: He is old. How long can he still live?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.histor: Ohh, I wish it were that straightforward, and that one could simply prove things false or true, yet the real world teems with discrepancies and truths untold, and everything you can learn will brim chock-full with details that are not known, and that shall never be known, to be left inconsistent, incoherent, contradictory, for the history of the world is written not in prose,|| but in poetry.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.how_.o: How do I talk to Him?
How do I reach His depths?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.how___: How can I trust that what you say is true?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.i.opti: I shall not commit the same mistakes.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.i__.op: I think I'll keep it for the time being.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.i___.o: I... I must have heard the title somewhere and forgot, then.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.i_____: I find it discomfiting, frankly.
I have already gone through it.
I shall try nonetheless.
I find this "Gnawing Sensation" to be not as relevant as a literal voice telling me it was... §omyself§r.
I am sorry...
I... never thought it possible. I shall heed your warning. Thank you.
I drew it myself on the pages of Al Azif. I don't recall seeing it previously.
I drew it myself on the pages of the Necronomicon. I don't recall seeing it previously.
I understand.
I see; but, perhaps, that is not a bad thing.
I do not think her capable of regret.
I would have fought against them.
I would have been too afraid, I admit.
I... do not know.
I shall try nonetheless.
I hope so too.
I don't understand.
I... am frightened.
I do not fear death.
I see.
I understand. Thank you.
I see. That substance common to all life.
I am sorry to hear.
I understand you have suffered much, but your plight is never mentioned in our history books. What can you tell me about it?
I understand you have suffered much, but your plight is never mentioned in our history books. What can you tell me about it?
I wish to talk about gods.
I wish to talk about symbols.
I wish to talk about you shoremen.
I do not remember.
I... am frightened.
I wish to talk about gods.
I wish to talk about symbols.
I wish to talk about you shoremen.
I understand.
I don't understand.
I don't understand.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.if.opt: If I seek to learn the history of the world, I want my sources to be reliable. Would you read a book that was proven false?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.immort:
We saw it in our dreams. We knew that, had you not come in peace, we would have had no escape – even if we killed you.
And we remember, for we had faced one such as you before.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.indeed: Indeed he was the first to accept me.
Indeed. Thank you for the enlightening conversation.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.inhabi: And how! All manners of creatures, thinking and not, some of which even rose to the surface and... er...
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.inpers:
I do not know of anyone who has, though I am sure some must have tried.
But then again, to what end? Nothing but dreams can breach it, and, even if you did venture into the deepest abyss, you would gaze disappointedly towards that last door, with no key in hand.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.is.opt: Is it too late to go back?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.it.opt: It should not have happened. None of it.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.it_.op: It is what anyone should have done.
It was indeed through dreams that I was brought to you.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.keeper:
At the top of the lighthouse, he can see everything.
He is the town elder, wisest among us, and the only one who still remembers the old ways. Everyone seeks his counsel.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.late.0:
I am afraid so. I am afraid §oto§r.
But, with you at my side, I can yet share my knowledge, and that comforts me enough.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.life.0: Er... yes. Except the life inhabiting those very depths, of course. They predate blood.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.listen:
Whether He listens or not, I cannot say – nor do I know what "listening" would even entail for something such as Him – but as for responding... no. He does not.
After all, why would he? To Him we are naught but dust. He is... indifferent.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.love.o: Love is the hallmark of a good leader. You are fortunate.
Indeed. And I hope that it never crumbles, and that we never find two axioms to simply... contradict each other.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.necron: That is... a morbid title, yet strangely alluring.
Then it was really... really Him... oh, what have you gotten yourself into...
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.no:.op: No: I am the one who should be grateful to you, for opening my mind beyond the dogmas I used to believe in.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.noreme:
Like a dream. I understand.
I shall enquire into any similar occurrences, among the shoremen and the world at large, and shall let you know of any findings. You did well to tell me.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.norepl:
I see, but do not worry! You do indeed seem changed, and if you survived the ritual, then, certainly, it must have worked.
I thus welcome thee among us once more, dear traveller; and let the Ocean's blessing be upon thee.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.notsay: Do not worry – I understand any reticence in sharing. I understand it very well.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.nowher: Nowhere, actually. It came to me right now.

You keep surprising me – all these things, coming to your mind, as if from nowhere.
This symbol, however, is a terrible one: whereas the Eye of Knowledge symbolises mankind's freedom to seek their path to knowledge, this, the §oTendril of Knowledge§r, reflects a much bleaker philosophy.

It was adopted by a fringe movement among occult researchers – a sect within a sect – who dispute the notion of free will as applied to us Seekers of Truth, proclaiming instead that our journey towards knowledge is guided by some external force, as if snatched by a tendril against our consent.
I advise you not to draw that symbol. The movement was deemed heretic long ago, and even now the debate over its legitimacy remains heated. Blood was shed, and I would not want to lose you.
The Ocean is our medium: a Veil before the Veil, this immense bulwark concealing the void. It is through water that our dreams travel down, and so the Ocean forwards our prayers to the Dreamer.
He is, thus, our Archpriest, whom we also call Dagon, and who rules the depths for He §ois§r the depths.

He is also our Father, senior to the stars and eldest in the universe, who spawned all life from water. Academia refers to Him as Arche, that is, the §oFirst Principle§r, whose turmoils originated the world.
And so he has been an object of study in all of mankind's history: first, by philosophers; then, by warlocks; now, even by scientists.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.old.0: However long Father Ocean lets him.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.one.op: One claimed to be the Gnawing Sensation at the back of my mind. The other claimed to be... me?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.oursel: Hah, good question, as proven by the fact that I do not know the answer. But that is a question for someone wiser than me, for knowing one's self is within the scope of wisdom, and not erudition.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.perhap: Perhaps that day shall come sooner rather than later.
Perhaps... but she was never found. She may still be alive, grieving her beloved.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.plight:
Deleted from the annals, oh the shame on those who wrote them!
Yes, our people were slain by yours, and some of us are old enough to remember it. Crusades, inquisitions, persecutions, witch hunts – whatever you want to call them, we were the monsters they hunted. We... normal men.
So how can one blame us when we mistrust outsiders? Were a whole people to come and take your family, how could you not be suspicious of anyone from that same population!
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.pray.0:
I guess... I guess we do it for ourselves. Like all worldly religions, in truth. To strengthen our belief in the only thing we know: that we cannot know.
It is a comforting feeling, that of recognising our limits, the small island that is the human mind.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.pray.1: And, of course, though the Veil remains silent, the Ocean does not – and to Him, and to His children, we do speak.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.precon: Yes. Perhaps one day we shall find a race from another world, and we shall compare our sciences, and we shall learn whether there is one true, objective science, or if even that is unreachable.
You must descend the vessels of the world, the very veins bringing detritus down below – for the chain tying life and water is that red fluid you call blood.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.replie:
Then you too heard His voice, soothing, consoling, the crashing of waves upon your mind, as you drowned... and then breathed.
I thus welcome thee among us once more, dear traveller; and let the Ocean's blessing be upon thee.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.schola: I did my time in academia, long ago. My research lay at the intersection between cultural studies and theology: I investigated the interactions between radically different communities, particularly my own with all others, and put a special focus in the role of faith in such contrasts.

I even held a professorship at the Miskatonic for a time, though I was denied tenure. Eventually I no longer felt welcome, and the ethics department started branding my research as "unethical". Before I reached my thirty-fifth year of age, the inquisitions began, and all was lost.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.scienc:
Hmm, perhaps you are right. At least within the frame of our world, and even despite the flaws of academia – of which, I assure you, there are several – science has the greatest prospect of being "true". Though it may depend on the science.
You know, I have always had a fascination towards mathematics: queen among sciences, boldly confident in the truth of its theorems and corollaries.
Science is objective. All (respectable) scientific knowledge is proven, by logic or experiment.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.sentie: "We" is all living creatures, so confident in our role as kings of the universe that we believe to have exclusive claim on sentience. The Great Dreamer is sentient, that is certain... but he does not §olive§r. He is more than that.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.shorem: I see. We have a short but vivid history, one we wish the outside world would learn.
Slugs are little reminders of what life is like at the bottom of the sea; amorphous, inchoate, mirroring the turmoils of currents in those depths.
They are our means of communion, through which we speak to the ocean.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.snippe: A snippet of truth, and so the Veil tears a little.
Yes, and, perhaps, He may even answer. However, be forewarned: the Ocean is multifaceted, and though He may appear calm and placid on one occasion, He may well be raging and thunderous on the next – do not seek to understand Him; He is beyond us.
Esteemed friend, shall we meet and debate the state of humankind? Please, let me share all that I know.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.stubbo: Haha! Then perhaps stubbornness has become a virtue!
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.surger: ...then you would find blood and guts. Do not go on that terrible path, I beg of you.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.symbol: Symbols and emblems are widespread among researchers of the occult, often identifying one's affiliation and school of thought. Are there any you are interested in discussing?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.tendri: Huh? Where did you see this?
Thank you. I look forward to it.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.thank_: Thank you. I shall remain cautious.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.then.o: Then I shall reach it through dreams.
Then each such truth is part of one greater truth, and were each being to describe its own view, its own history, the course of its life, then we would finally see it all as a whole, unmarred by bias: all sides of a war, all angles of an event, all joy and all pain.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.then__: Then I shall reach it through dreams.
Then it adequately mirrors its content.
Then let us hope for His blessing.
Then again, even science is founded upon our preconceived notions of the world.
There is a Veil within each and every one of us, as you said, and if I were to dig deep into our own bodies...
These §odeep creatures§r you refer to... did they fight for you?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.throug: Then perhaps our dreams embraced each other, dancing unseen in their own little world.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.true.0: This is the only true answer: one cannot judge how they would have acted when in a different state of mind, and like the sleepy man should not cancel plans for the next day, so too should the safe man not guess his actions in a perilous situation.
What do you mean "is true"?! How would I know if it is true or not!
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.two.op: Two more voices in my head, but I did not talk back.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.twomor:
This... this is unprecedented. I truly do not know what to say...
...but what did §othey§r say? When they spoke to you? Who were they??
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.unders: Neither do I. I shall enquire into any similar occurrences, among the shoremen and the world at large, and shall let you know of any findings. You did well to tell me.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.veil.0: One could start pondering the ways in which a given nomenclature alters the frame through which we envision things, and how referring to a "Dreamer" may make the object more human and sympathetic than an abstract "Veil" – but yes, essentially they are one and the same.
The Veil is all things that conceal a truth, of any sort.
The Veil is the curtain of a theatre, divorcing stage from audience.
The Veil is prejudice, and mankind's unwillingness to peek beyond it.

The Veil is language, and the words we speak, unable to capture the true essence of things.
The Veil is your skin, your face, your persona, and everything concealing your true self.
The Veil is death, that final threshold through which we cannot see.

The Veil is a great many things, and it resides deep down below, at the bottom of the sea. It is the great bulwark concealing infinity, the truth to all answers.
We call Him the Great Dreamer, for only dreams may pass through Him.
What is the Ocean to you?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.what__: What is the Ocean to you?
What role does the lighthouse keeper have?
What role did the lighthouse keeper have?
What is the Ocean to you?
What role does the lighthouse keeper have?
What role did the lighthouse keeper have?
What do these concentric circles represent?
What do the slugs mean to you shoremen?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.whatel: Is there anything else you wish to speak about?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.yellin: I'm sorry! I'm sorry, sorry, sorry! It's that... oh, what have you gotten yourself into...
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.you.0: Yes, that too is astonishing... oh, what have you gotten yourself into...
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.you.op: You are right. I cannot say.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.you_.o: You will never have to fear me.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.discuss.you___: You couldn't be acquainted with it: it is the diary and journal upon which I note down my findings.
You are right. I cannot blame you.
You can never generalize from the actions of a few. There is conflict within every population, and just as many may have been for it as against.
You are right. Maths is beautiful.
You are right. The real truth is impenetrable.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.initial.alas.0:
Alas, our customs dictate I do not discuss my learnings with outsiders.
I am sorry.
A dream? Of our black shores and dark water?
I see. Perhaps, then, our dreams met already. Deep down below, at the bottom of the ocean.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.initial.just.o: Just like the others, blinded by prejudice.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.initial.knowle:
Ah, I understand.
Indeed, I spy in your eyes that burning flame of curiosity, searing idleness away and leading you into that incessant march of learning.
I can well grasp your struggle. I, too, am a scholar, and oh the wonders I have seen and dreamt of!
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.initial.no_.op: No. I was brought here by a dream.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.initial.prejud:
Prejudice? I.. I...
..I see. You have given me food for thought, traveller.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.initial.someon: Someone who does not view me with the stern eyes of suspicion.
Oh? Good day, dear pilgrim. Are you in search of something, here in our humble dwelling?
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.initial.suspic:
I see. Please understand that it is not a mere lack of trust. In view of an outsider, our hearts are gripped with fear. By instinct, almost.
Yet it is not innate. There is much history behind our demeanor.
Much fury. Much grief. Much regret.
dialogue.shoreman_scholar.initial1.start:
Alas, our customs dictate I do not discuss my learnings with outsiders.
I am sorry.
doskill.doclimbing.description:
This skill, when toggled on, enables you to jump upwards when adjacent to walls, effectively letting you easily scale vertical surfaces. When you are in mid air and near a solid block, you'll be able to jump off of it - this can be done repeatedly as long as you're near a scalable surface.
Unfortunately, this skill does not work in the arena. There's no escaping the challenge.
doskill.doclimbing.name: Climbing
doskill.dohealth.description: A straightforward, passive ability, when enabled this skill simply grants extra health to your Deep One form.
doskill.dohealth.name: Extra Health
doskill.dopoison.description:
When toggled on this skill gives you a chance to inject enemies with a powerful poison when you hit them. About one in four hits result in the effect being applied.
It does not, unfortunately, work against bosses.
doskill.dopoison.name: Poison Claws
doskill.doquickstep.description:
When enabled, this skill lets you do quick sidesteps by pressing the Dodge Key ('Alt' by default). Like all skills, it requires you to be in your Deep One form. The direction of the dodge depends on the currently pressed movement keys (WASD).
When used skillfully, the quickstep can speedily bring one out of danger, escaping perilous attacks. It may not, however, be used underwater or in mid air.
doskill.doquickstep.name: Quickstep
doskill.doregeneration.description: When enabled, this skill gives you a regeneration effect every time you slay an enemy. The more powerful the felled creature, the greater the effect. Very weak creatures however might result in no effect being applied.
doskill.doregeneration.name: Corpse Feeding
doskill.doroarsink.description:
When enabled, the Sinking Roar empowers your normal roar by violently sending downwards any waterborne creatures affected by it. Additionally, if the creatures possess a limited air supply (like Players do) the roar will also knock all the air out of their lungs, making them immediately start drowning.
Of course, several enemies are quite resistant to your roars: bosses, Ictya, and other creatures more fearsome than you may be entirely unaffected.
doskill.doroarsink.name: Sinking Roar
doskill.douppercut.description:
Whereas the quickstep puts distance between you and the enemy by moving you away from it, the uppercut achieves the same result by launching your adversary up in the air, with potentially fatal results in the subsequent landing. This skill, when toggled on, lets you right click on other creatures while barehanded to send them flying upwards.
It does, however, become less effective against powerful enemies with large health pools, as well as bosses. It is also of little use in water combat.
doskill.douppercut.name: Uppercut
doskill.unlock.deep_one_brute: - A Deep One Brute must be slain to unlock this skill.
doskill.unlock.deep_one_myrmidon: - A Deep One Myrmidon must be slain to unlock this skill.
doskill.unlock.large: - A new large sized Ictya must be slain to unlock this skill.
doskill.unlock.medium: - A new medium sized Ictya must be slain to unlock this skill.
dreams.alienis.almostthere1: I am certain this aspect will be of great aid very soon.
dreams.alienis.almostthere2: But now is not the time. I lack the strength.
dreams.alienis.needvacuos: As of now, I need to have dreamt of the Void for this to work.

dreams.deathsearch.toostrong: %1$s's mind is too well shielded to be breached.
